name: Basic Crispy Potato Skins
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 400°F. Scrub the potatoes clean and pierce each one several times with a fork. Bake for 45 minutes to 1 hour, or until tender.
Cut each potato lengthwise into 4 quarters. Scoop out the potato pulp from the skins, leaving a 1/8 inch thick shell. Save the pulp for another recipe or use it below.
Brush the potato skins inside and out with melted butter. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Place the skins on a baking sheet, skin-side down. Bake for about 15 minutes or until crisp. Serve hot. Makes 2 servings.
FOR LEFTOVER POTATO INSIDES:
Mashed potatoes: Add your choice of salt, pepper, sliced green onion, crumbled bacon, shredded cheese, and/or fresh herbs. Top with bread crumbs, dot with butter, and bake until heated through.
Saute chopped onion in butter until tender. Stir in the potato pulp and continue to cook and stir until heated through.
Make a potato salad using the leftover potato pulp. Toss with bottled vinegar dressing or mayonnaise, sliced green onion, chopped olives, and crumbled bacon. Chill.
Add to soups to thicken them.
